title: Manage Time Perception: - Practice Calmness: Breathe Deeply to Slow Things Down When You
subtitle: Master Your Perception of Time
description:
To manage time perception:
- Practice calmness: Breathe deeply to slow things down when you feel rushed.
- Focus intently: In critical moments, focus on one detail at a time to create clarity.
- Review after action: Reflect on what happened to separate emotions from actual timing.

Example: During a stressful meeting, focus on your breath and slow your response to regain control.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify your current sense of time under pressure. Log how long stressful moments feel compared to actual duration. This will help you measure improvements.

howTo.title: Choose your anchors
howTo.summary: Select a tactile object and a two-word phrase to use as anchors during stressful situations. These will serve as reminders to practice your breathing technique.

howTo.title: Practice the micro-anchor
howTo.summary: When feeling rushed, use your anchors to trigger a 60-90 second breathing exercise. Focus on your breath and a single detail to regain clarity.

howTo.title: Conduct the 3-day experiment
howTo.summary: Over three days, implement your anchors in real situations. Log your control minutes and felt intensity to track progress and adjust your approach.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app. Reflect on your experiences and adjust your anchors and practices as needed. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/slow-time-under-pressure/.
